channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Online backlash emerges over UFC stage design for White House Freedom 250 event
item.description: A UFC stage design set up for an upcoming event at the White House has sparked widespread online criticism and ridicule. The UFC Freedom 250 stage is being constructed on the South Lawn in Washington,...

item.title: Rare 1935 Bugatti Type 57 ‘Grand Raid’ Roadster Showcases Pre-War Automotive Design
item.description: The article highlights the elegance and historical significance of the 1935 Bugatti Type 57 ‘Grand Raid’ Roadster, one of the most admired luxury sports cars from the pre-war automotive era. Designed ...

item.title: Florrie Holmes and the Forty Elephants: Women of London’s Notorious All-Female Crime Gang
item.description: The article presents a historical portrait of Florrie Holmes, one of the known members of the Forty Elephants, an all-female criminal gang that operated in London between 1873 and the 1950s. Based in ...

item.title: Antonelli wins Canadian Grand Prix as Russell retires after Mercedes power unit failure
item.description: Kimi Antonelli secured a dominant victory at the Formula 1 Canadian Grand Prix after Mercedes teammate George Russell retired from the race due to a power unit failure. The race at Circuit Gilles Vill...

item.title: KBA Opposes Proposed 16 Percent VAT on Mobile Money Fees Over Fears of Reduced Digital Transactions
item.description: The Kenya Bankers Association (KBA) has opposed a proposal in the Finance Bill 2026 that seeks to introduce a 16 percent VAT on charges imposed by mobile money and digital payment platforms such as M-...

item.title: Early Christmas Cover Art from The New Yorker in the 1920s and 1930s
item.description: The article presents a retrospective look at 51 Christmas-themed covers from The New Yorker magazine, focusing on works produced during the 1920s and 1930s. Founded in 1925 by Harold Ross and Jane Gra...

item.title: U.S. Military Targets Iranian Missile Facilities and Boats Near Strait of Hormuz Following Attacks on Naval Ships
item.description: The United States carried out military strikes against Iranian facilities near Bandar Abbas after Iranian forces allegedly attacked U.S. warships operating in the Strait of Hormuz. According to report...

item.title: White House Prepares Large UFC Event Amid Ongoing Construction and Concerns About Insects
item.description: Preparations for the upcoming UFC Freedom 250 event at the White House have drawn attention as construction activity expands across the grounds ahead of the celebration planned for June 14, 2026. Acco...

item.title: A Look Back at Nastassja Kinski’s International Film Career in the 1970s and 1980s
item.description: The article highlights the career and cultural impact of German actress and former model Nastassja Kinski, who became one of the most recognizable international film stars of the late 1970s and 1980s....

item.title: Frank Sinatra’s Early Life and Childhood in Hoboken, New Jersey
item.description: The article explores the early life and childhood of Frank Sinatra, one of the most influential American singers of the 20th century, through rare photographs and biographical details. Sinatra was bor...

item.title: Ontario regulator reviews MAID practices following concerns over assessment location and clinical errors
item.description: A London, Ontario physician is facing increased scrutiny from the College of Physicians and Surgeons of Ontario (CPSO) following two separate complaints involving Medical Assistance in Dying (MAID) ca...

item.title: John Bolton says Iran is exploiting Trump’s urgency for a negotiated deal
item.description: Former U.S. National Security Advisor John Bolton warned that Iran is taking advantage of what he describes as President Donald Trump’s strong desire to reach a negotiated agreement in ongoing tension...

item.title: Ursula Andress in Hammer’s 1965 Fantasy Film 'She' and Its Production Legacy
item.description: The article focuses on the 1965 British adventure-fantasy film She, produced by Hammer Film Productions and adapted from H. Rider Haggard’s 1887 novel of the same name. The film is highlighted as a vi...

item.title: Report Claims JD Vance Is Reconsidering a 2028 Presidential Campaign Amid Foreign Policy Disputes
item.description: A report published by The Daily Mail and highlighted by Raw Story claims that Vice President JD Vance is facing increasing political isolation within President Donald Trump’s administration and may be...

item.title: Early 20th-Century Studio Portraits Featuring Artificial Snow Effects
item.description: The article explores a popular photographic trend from the early 20th century in which studio portraits were designed to simulate snowy winter scenes. At a time when outdoor photography was often diff...

item.title: Manufacturers warn Finance Bill 2026 proposals could raise production costs in Kenya
item.description: The Kenya Association of Manufacturers (KAM) has raised concerns over the Finance Bill 2026, warning that several proposed tax and levy changes may significantly increase production costs across Kenya...

item.title: Photo Captures John Lennon and Yoko Ono Interrupting Their 1969 Peace Protest for Hotel Housekeeping
item.description: The article revisits a well-known moment from John Lennon and Yoko Ono’s 1969 “Bed-In for Peace” protest in Amsterdam, where the couple used their honeymoon as a public demonstration against war and v...

item.title: Thomas Massie Signals Possible 2028 Political Return After Losing GOP Primary
item.description: Kentucky Representative Thomas Massie, a Republican known for frequently breaking with his party leadership and President Donald Trump, is signaling that he may not be done with electoral politics des...

item.title: WHO warns worsening Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda is outpacing response efforts
item.description: World Health Organization Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus warned that the Ebola outbreak affecting the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda is spreading rapidly and may worsen before im...

item.title: Woman dies in hospital after shooting outside Sheffield city centre bar
item.description: A 30-year-old woman has died after being shot outside the One Four One bar on West Street in Sheffield city centre during the early hours of Monday morning. Emergency services were called to the scene...
